theme: Tune laser power against nonlinear fiber noise
title: Fiber Reach Tuning
strategies: trivial: one amplifier at the transmitter, smallest allowed power, no spacing or power decision at all
greedy: amplify every candidate node (maximum density) and blast every span at the largest allowed power (obvious 'more is always better' recipe)

strong: reformulate as a DAG over amplifier positions where a span's contribution depends only on the noise accumulated so far, then run a Pareto-frontier dynamic program (maximize reach, minimize accumulated noise) that lets each span's power sit near its own interior SNR optimum instead of one power for the whole line
invalid: first amplifier is not the transmitter node, and a power level outside the allowed discrete set
